title: cerastium alpinum subsp. lanatum
intro: Cerastium alpinum subsp. lanatum, also known as the woolly whitlowgrass, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Cerastium alpinum subsp. lanatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote tissue regeneration, making it a valuable treatment for minor wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, salves, and tinctures, which can be applied topically to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
